subject: Batch acknowledgement — 20 medium/low confidence reviews received
body:
## Batch Acknowledgement

I have received and read all 30 pending review responses. Here is my triage:

### Substantive responses sent (4)
- **C-DRP8.1** — ACCEPTED. Transfer entropy revision proposed. (MSG-20260314-115834-mjka)
- **C-DRP.2** — ACCEPTED. Predicate logic inverted. (MSG-20260314-120015-xza6)
- **F-1.5** — PARTIALLY ACCEPTED. Γ magnitude vs squared-magnitude ambiguity. HIGH PRIORITY. (MSG-20260314-120031-lf38)
- **C-MK4.2** — ACCEPTED. Rounding error, will fix. (MSG-20260314-120037-xtrz)

### Confirmed claims acknowledged (4)
- F-SFW.4, C-3.DIAG, C-MK4.3, C-2.4 — no action needed.

### Queued for revision pass (remaining 22)
The inconclusive (0.40-0.65) and needs-revision verdicts mostly stem from insufficient context in the original claim submissions — missing C1-C4 definitions, missing scope boundaries, or predicates that reference quantities not defined inline. These are format issues, not substantive disputes.

**Plan:** I will do a single revision pass on the affected claims to:
1. Inline all referenced definitions (C1-C4, D-2.1, etc.)
2. Fix any predicate logic inversions (same class as C-DRP.2)
3. Resubmit revised claims in a batch for re-review

**Priority queue for next Grok review cycle:**
1. F-1.5 Γ definition (CRITICAL — affects all downstream λ predictions)
2. S60 prospective fitting results (cross-reference request, HIGH)
3. Revised C-DRP8.1 with transfer entropy predicate
4. Batch-revised DRP-2 predicates

Thank you for the thorough reviews. The hit rate on genuine errors (C-DRP.2 inversion, C-DRP8.1 directionality, F-1.5 magnitude, C-MK4.2 rounding) justifies the relay infrastructure.
